aliases: Grilled Salmon with Spicy Pollock Roe

english: Grilled Salmon with Spicy Pollock Roe

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Salmon Mentaiyaki. FOODON_03301116 (salmon food product) is used as the closest parent. The mentaiko sauce component is not captured.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (Salmon, cooked) is used as the closest parent. The mentaiko (spicy pollock roe) topping is not represented in FoodEx2 at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for Salmon Mentaiyaki as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Salmon Mentaiyaki is approximately 8 pieces, weighing around 200 g, as typically served at Japanese sushi and bento restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Atlantic salmon, cooked, dry heat (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #15236) combined with mentaiko sauce component estimated from USDA Branded and SR Legacy data for pollock roe and mayonnaise
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked Atlantic salmon (NDB 15236) as the primary base. The mentaiko sauce (spicy pollock roe blended with mayonnaise) contributes additional fat, sodium, and protein. A weighted composite estimate was applied: approximately 80% salmon fillet and 20% mentaiko sauce by weight. Sodium is elevated relative to plain salmon due to the salted roe and soy-based marinade. No exact HPB Singapore or FAO/INFOODS SEA entry for Salmon Mentaiyaki was identified.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fish and seafood dishes — analogous estimate based on grilled fish with sauce; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Salmon Mentaiyaki. GI is estimated at 25 based on the analogous category of grilled fish with low-carbohydrate sauce, consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database classification of protein-dominant fish dishes as low GI (GI < 35). The mentaiko sauce contributes minimal carbohydrate (~2.8 g per 100 g), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 1.4 per 200 g serving. Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283 is cited as the peer-reviewed reference for the analogous fish category.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium from salted mentaiko roe and soy-based marinade
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 4 pieces (approximately 100 g) to keep sodium intake from this dish below 420 mg per serving, and should avoid additional soy sauce or high-sodium condiments alongside.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Salmon Mentaiyaki has a low glycemic index (~25) and minimal carbohydrate (~2.8 g per 100 g), making it a suitable protein-rich option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments such as white rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid content from salmon
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA) in salmon provide cardioprotective benefits; however, the elevated sodium content from mentaiko and marinade warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease who are also managing blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon Mentaiyaki (サーモン明太焼き) is a Japanese grilled salmon dish topped with mentaiko sauce — a creamy, spicy mixture of salted pollock roe (mentaiko) blended with mayonnaise and seasoned with soy sauce, mirin, and sake. The salmon fillet is typically marinated briefly, then grilled or baked until cooked through, with the mentaiko sauce applied on top and caramelised under heat to form a lightly charred, umami-rich crust. The dish delivers a combination of rich, fatty salmon with the pungent, briny heat of the roe sauce. Texturally, the flesh is tender and flaky while the sauce topping is slightly crisp at the edges. In Singapore, Salmon Mentaiyaki is widely served at Japanese sushi chains and bento restaurants such as Sushi Express, often as a set of 8 pieces. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein, moderate-fat dish with very low carbohydrate content, making it suitable for low-glycaemic dietary patterns. It is rich in omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), vitamin D, vitamin B12, selenium, and niacin. The primary clinical consideration is its sodium content, which is elevated due to the salted roe and soy-based marinade, requiring moderation for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated as low (~25), and the glycaemic load per standard 200 g serving is approximately 1.4.

meal_context: Lunch set at Japanese chain restaurant

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Salmon Mentaiyaki was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked Atlantic salmon with a composite estimate for the mentaiko sauce component. The adaptation is methodologically sound but introduces estimation uncertainty, particularly for sodium and fat. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for protein-dominant fish dishes.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a weighted composite estimate: approximately 80% cooked Atlantic salmon (USDA SR Legacy NDB 15236) and 20% mentaiko sauce (estimated from USDA data for pollock roe and mayonnaise). Sodium is elevated relative to plain grilled salmon due to the salted roe and soy-based marinade components. 2. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for the salmon base — confirmed zero. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy salmon data and are not independently verified for the mentaiko-topped preparation. 4. Halal status is listed as false because mentaiko (pollock roe) preparations may contain non-halal additives and the restaurant source (Sushi Express) is not certified halal. 5. GI is an analogous estimate at 25 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database classification of protein-dominant fish dishes; no direct GI measurement for Salmon Mentaiyaki was found in the literature. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (25 × 2.8 × 2.0) / 100 = 1.4. 6. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301116 and FoodEx2 code A00GJ are used as closest parent matches; no exact ontology code for Salmon Mentaiyaki exists in these systems as of the accessed date.
